terms.definition: The graph of loss L(θ) over model parameters θ; each parameter setting is a point and L(θ) is its height on the landscape.
Synonym for loss surface—the geometric terrain of loss values over parameter space, often used in deep-learning literature.
A parameter point where loss is lower than at all nearby points but not necessarily the lowest globally on the surface.
A parameter point achieving the lowest loss over the entire feasible parameter space.
A critical point where the gradient is zero but loss decreases in some directions and increases in others—not a true minimum.
A region of the loss surface where loss changes slowly and gradients are small, causing optimization to stall without careful step-size control.
A 2D map of level curves where each curve connects parameter points with equal loss—a top-down view of a loss surface slice.
